project.name: Vayl
project.description: A native iOS app for couples that pairs two devices for shared intimacy and relationship experiences, built around an elaborate animated onboarding canvas, a safe-worded two-device Card Session, and a Desire Map matching feature. The SwiftUI client (Observable @MainActor Store architecture, SwiftData persistence, Metal/SpriteKit visual effects) is backed by Supabase, with Deno/TypeScript edge functions for pairing, partner lookup, desire matching, and entitlements, plus Postgres migrations.

nodes.name: 20260101000000_baseline.sql
nodes.summary: Baseline schema migration that provisions the entire Vayl backend: extensions (pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_cron), helper functions, and the 10 core tables (user_profiles, couples, pairing_codes, curated_sessions, desire_ratings, desire_matches, card_progress, assessment_responses/results, couple_session_records) with RLS policies scoped to couple membership.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an rls_auto_enable event trigger to force row-level security on every newly created public table; all read/write policies gate on user_profiles.auth_id = auth.uid() or couple membership.

nodes.name: user_profiles
nodes.summary: Per-user profile keyed by auth_id, storing onboarding state, NM flavor/stage, pairing code, couple linkage, orientation and role preferences. The pivot table linking Supabase auth identities to couple-scoped data.

nodes.name: couples
nodes.summary: Couple record joining two user_profiles (user_a, user_b) with a shared safe word and a matches_revealed flag; the central scoping entity that most RLS policies and foreign keys reference.

nodes.name: pairing_codes
nodes.summary: Short-lived invite codes for two-device pairing, tracking creator, expiry, and who claimed the code into which couple. RLS allows the creator to manage their row and a non-creator to claim it.

nodes.name: curated_sessions
nodes.summary: Two-device curated card-session state: deck/variant, card list, per-card and global timers, per-partner presence/bandwidth/consent flags, reveal state, and safe-word usage. A unique partial index enforces one open session per couple.

nodes.name: desire_ratings
nodes.summary: Per-user rating of an individual desire_item, the raw input that feeds Desire Map match computation.

nodes.name: desire_matches
nodes.summary: Computed alignment between partners on a desire item: alignment level, each partner's value, gap size, and an optional bridge card. The couple-facing output of the Desire Map.

nodes.name: card_progress
nodes.summary: Per-couple status of each card (e.g. discussed/skipped), used to track deck progression across a couple's sessions.

nodes.name: couple_session_records
nodes.summary: Historical log of completed couple sessions with start/end timestamps and count of cards discussed.

nodes.name: assessment_responses
nodes.summary: Per-user raw answers to onboarding assessment questions, keyed by question_key.

nodes.name: assessment_results
nodes.summary: Per-user derived assessment results (result_key/result_value pairs) computed from assessment_responses.

nodes.name: 20260616223000_p5d_revoke_anon_default_privileges.sql
nodes.summary: Security-hardening migration (P5d) that revokes the anon role from default privileges on future tables, functions, and sequences in the public schema, and drops a residual anon EXECUTE grant on set_curated_sessions_updated_at(), closing the future-object exposure gap left by P5b.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES so that newly created objects no longer auto-grant ALL to anon — a defense-in-depth complement to per-table RLS.

nodes.name: 20260617000000_desire_map_backend.sql
nodes.summary: Desire Map backend migration that creates the desire_map_status table (per-couple track + per-partner completion + full-reveal gating) and extends desire_ratings (rating check constraint) and desire_matches (is_free_reveal, revealed_at) to support the reveal flow.

nodes.name: desire_map_status
nodes.summary: Per-couple Desire Map progress: which track, each partner's completion timestamp, whether the full reveal is unlocked, and a waiting-state marker. Drives the gated reveal experience.

nodes.name: 20260617120000_monetization_entitlements.sql
nodes.summary: Monetization migration that creates the service-role-only entitlements ledger (couple_id, product_id, is_active, purchased_by) with RLS and a couple_id index, and adds the denormalized access_tier / core_unlocked_at / is_founding_member cache columns to couples.
nodes.languageNotes: The entitlements table is deliberately never client-readable (purchased_by is support-only); tier is mirrored onto couples.access_tier for client reads while the ledger stays service-role-only.

nodes.name: entitlements
nodes.summary: Service-role-only purchase ledger granting a couple an entitlement (product_id, is_active, expires_at, purchased_by). Source of truth for the couple's paid access tier; never exposed to clients.

nodes.name: 20260617130000_entitlement_payer_portable_resolution.sql
nodes.summary: Defines two payer-portable resolution functions: resolve_couple_access_tier (reader returning 'core'/'free' from active entitlements matched by couple_id OR purchaser identity) and recompute_couple_entitlement (writer that refreshes the couples access_tier/founding/unlocked-at cache from the ledger), both locked to service_role.
nodes.languageNotes: Payer-portable design: an entitlement follows the buyer (purchased_by = user_a OR user_b), so re-pairing inherits Core. Functions are revoked from public/authenticated/anon and granted only to service_role.

nodes.name: assessment_questions.json
nodes.summary: Seed bank of ENM-readiness assessment questions (scale and multi-select), each tagged with a domain, weight, and sort order, consumed via ContentLoader.loadAssessmentQuestions(). Several entries are flagged as placeholders pending validated content.

nodes.name: cards.json
nodes.summary: Card-session content deck: education, prompt, and educationPrompt cards keyed to a category_id, with speaking-turn, difficulty, sensitivity, and free/paid gating. Loaded through ContentLoader.loadCards() to drive the couple card experience.

nodes.name: categories.json
nodes.summary: Category taxonomy for the Learn/card content (communication, boundaries, intimacy, etc.) with SF Symbol icons, phase grouping, card counts, and unlock gating. Loaded via ContentLoader.loadCategories() to organize and gate content sections.

nodes.name: desire_items.json
nodes.summary: Desire Map item catalog: per-item name, description, category, sensitivity, track membership (curious/established), and track-specific answer scales. Decoded into the DesireItem model via ContentLoader.loadDesireItems() to populate the Desire Map rater.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Supabase CLI config registering the lookup-code and create-pair edge functions with JWT verification enabled and their Deno import maps.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Deno edge function (service-role): marks a partner's Desire Map complete, resolves the couple track, and when both finish computes positive matches over shared items (excluding boundaries) into desire_matches with one server-authoritative free reveal.
nodes.languageNotes: isFreeReveal and the match write are server-authoritative so the paywall cannot be bypassed by the client.

nodes.name: matchType
nodes.summary: Classifies two partner ratings on a shared desire item into mutual (both excited), adjacent (any other positive pairing), or null (no match).

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: Placeholder npm registry configuration comment for the create-pair edge function's private package imports.

nodes.name: deno.json
nodes.summary: Deno import map for the create-pair edge function, aliasing the Supabase functions-js JSR package.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Deno edge function (service-role): validates a pairing code (existence, expiry, self-pair, prior-couple guards), creates a couples row, marks both profiles linked, and consumes the code.
nodes.languageNotes: Earlier variant that keys couples on the ids passed in the body; rapid-task is the profile-id-resolving successor used by the iOS app.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Deno edge function (service-role): returns only the linked partner's display name and pronouns for the caller's couple, since per-row RLS cannot expose cross-partner columns.
nodes.languageNotes: Column-scoped service-role read is the only path a partner's name reaches the client without exposing the rest of the row.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Deno edge function (service-role): the only path that grants Core to a couple, authorizing via Apple StoreKit receipt or an admin secret, writing the entitlements ledger, then recomputing the couple's access_tier.
nodes.languageNotes: Apple JWS verification is an M2 seam that fails closed (returns null) so the Apple path cannot bypass the paywall before it is hardened.

nodes.name: verifyAppleTransaction
nodes.summary: Fail-closed M1 stub for Apple StoreKit 2 signed-transaction (JWS) verification; returns null unless APPLE_VERIFICATION_ENABLED is set, deferring real x5c/ES256 verification to M2.

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: Placeholder npm registry configuration comment for the lookup-code edge function's private package imports.

nodes.name: deno.json
nodes.summary: Deno import map for the lookup-code edge function, aliasing the Supabase functions-js JSR package.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Deno edge function (service-role): validates a pairing code's existence and expiry and returns the inviter's display name and pronouns for the 'you're pairing with...' preview.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Deno edge function (slug rapid-task, the app's create-couple endpoint): claims a pairing code, resolves both partners' profile ids, creates the couples row, links both profiles, inherits any payer-portable entitlement, and deletes the code.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves user_profiles.id from auth ids before insert to satisfy the couples FK, fixing the earlier auth-id FK violation.

nodes.name: AppShell.swift

nodes.name: AppShell

nodes.name: SchemaV1

nodes.name: AppMigrationPlan

nodes.name: AppAnimation.swift

nodes.languageNotes: Provides reduceMotionSafe and an ambientAnimation modifier so every looping animation has a Reduce Motion fallback.

nodes.name: AppColors
nodes.summary: Struct of static color tokens spanning OB canvas, surfaces, text tiers, accents, feedback, shadows, and the cyan-purple-magenta spectrum gradient.

nodes.name: AppElevation.swift
nodes.summary: Elevation/shadow token namespace plus cardElevation/modalElevation view modifiers that apply the correct shadow stack for the current color scheme.

nodes.name: AppElevation
nodes.summary: Enum namespace defining card and modal shadow elevation tokens and the OB physics cardShadow(elevation:) ramp.

nodes.name: AppFonts.swift
nodes.summary: Typography token system mapping semantic roles (heroTitle, cardTitle, bodyText, ctaLabel) to the ClashDisplay and Switzer faces with display/body constructors for custom sizes.

nodes.name: AppFonts
nodes.summary: Struct of semantic font tokens over the ClashDisplay (display) and Switzer (body) families plus dynamic-type-aware constructors.

nodes.name: AppGlows.swift
nodes.summary: Glow-effect token namespace and view modifiers (spectrumBorderGlow, cornerDeckGlow, safetyGlow) so views express glows through tokens instead of raw shadows.

nodes.name: AppGlows
nodes.summary: Enum namespace holding the app's spectrum, corner-deck, accent-focus, and safety glow parameters consumed by the AppGlows view modifiers.

nodes.name: AppGrid.swift
nodes.summary: Layout-grid token struct defining column counts, gutters, and grid math used to align content to the app's spacing system.

nodes.name: AppGrid
nodes.summary: Struct of grid tokens (columns, gutters, margins) providing the structural layout grid for app surfaces.

nodes.name: AppLayout.swift
nodes.summary: GeometryProxy-derived layout resolver exposing screen/card/safe-area metrics and the mandatory OB and session card sizing helpers, replacing all UIScreen.main usage.
nodes.languageNotes: AppLayout.from(geo) is the iOS-26-compliant replacement for UIScreen.main.bounds; OB/session card sizes are all proportional helpers.

nodes.name: AppLayout
nodes.summary: Struct resolved from a GeometryProxy that vends screen/card/safe-area dimensions plus static OB card, fan, and session card sizing functions.

nodes.name: AppRadius.swift
nodes.summary: Semantic corner-radius token namespace (micro through sheet, plus OB-only obCard/cornerCard/foilEdge) that every clip and rounded-rectangle must reference.

nodes.name: AppRadius
nodes.summary: Enum namespace of corner-radius tokens on a 4pt grid, including device-matched sheet corners and OB-canvas-exclusive card radii.

nodes.name: AppRootView.swift
nodes.summary: Top-level routing gate that shows the splash once per cold launch, then reactively routes to onboarding, sign-in, or AppShell based on AppState and AuthService.
nodes.languageNotes: Onboarding gates before auth because pairing's persistLink needs the local UserProfile that OnboardingStore.commit creates.

nodes.name: AppRootView
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View reading AuthService, AppState, and scenePhase to decide the post-splash destination and suppress splash replay on foreground resume.

nodes.name: AppSafeArea.swift
nodes.summary: Safe-area utilities: a real-safe-area environment key and AppLayout extension so views can read true device insets without UIScreen, with helper view modifiers.

nodes.name: AppSpacing.swift
nodes.summary: Semantic spacing token namespace (xxs through xxl on an 8pt grid) that every padding, gap, and offset must reference instead of raw numbers.

nodes.name: AppSpacing
nodes.summary: Enum namespace of CGFloat spacing tokens on an 8pt grid, the sole sanctioned source for padding and gap values.

nodes.name: AppTheme.swift
nodes.summary: Defines the ThemeMode enum (system/light/dark) retained for persistence after the app was hard-locked to dark and the legacy AppPalette was removed.

nodes.name: ThemeMode
nodes.summary: CaseIterable enum of theme modes with display names and SF Symbol icons, kept for settings persistence though the app forces dark.

nodes.name: DragDebugView.swift
nodes.summary: DEBUG-only isolated drag-gesture test view: a draggable circle plus an event log to confirm DragGesture fires and hit-testing is not blocked.
nodes.languageNotes: Wrapped in #if DEBUG with a #Preview for canvas-only use.

nodes.name: DragDebugView
nodes.summary: Debug View with a draggable circle driven by DragGesture and a rolling on-screen event log for diagnosing gesture/hit-testing issues.

nodes.name: AcknowledgementRecord.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Model recording that a user tapped through the 3-card ground-rules modal in The Opener; a device-only legal/ethical record with copy versioning.

nodes.name: AcknowledgementRecord
nodes.summary: @Model storing acknowledgement timestamp, copy version, and which ground-rule statements were tapped; computed isCurrent re-prompts when copy version increments.

nodes.name: Card.swift
nodes.summary: Read-only Codable content model for a single prompt card loaded from JSON, with derived flags (reveal mechanic, context beat, ceremonial, living) plus The Opener sample data.
nodes.languageNotes: Content layer is never stored in SwiftData; the String cardId is the only join to the progress layer.

nodes.name: Card
nodes.summary: Codable, Identifiable struct describing a prompt card (text, intensity, type, gendering, context beat) with derived booleans for reveal/ceremonial/living classification.

nodes.name: CardSession.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Models for deck-play tracking: couple-owned CardSession, per-card CardResult outcomes, and the unlinked-user SoloSession prep deck.

nodes.name: CardSession
nodes.summary: @Model for one couple-owned sitting with a deck — tracks pass number, attempt/discuss/skip/bookmark counts, Lock-In bandwidth, and cascades CardResult children.

nodes.name: CardResult
nodes.summary: @Model recording the outcome (discussed/skipped/bookmarked) of a single card within a session; String cardId joins to the JSON content layer.

nodes.name: SoloSession
nodes.summary: @Model for an unlinked user's 5-card solo prep deck session, owned by userId and retained as permanent history after a partner is linked.

nodes.name: CompanionCard.swift
nodes.summary: Stub Codable model for a Desire Map companion card that bridges a match into a next-step conversation prompt and optional suggested deck.

nodes.name: CompanionCard
nodes.summary: Codable, Identifiable, Hashable struct linking a DesireMatch (via bridgeCardId) to a conversation prompt and an optional suggested deck.

nodes.name: Couple.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Model for a linked partner connection — owns the shared safe word, entitlement tier, Desire Map reveal state, and cascading session/match/progress relationships.
nodes.languageNotes: Entitlement lives on Couple so one purchase covers both partners; dissolution archives rather than deletes.

nodes.name: Couple
nodes.summary: @Model holding the two partner IDs, connection plan, shared safe word, entitlement tier/unlock metadata, and cascade relationships to DesireMatch, CardSession, and DeckProgress.

nodes.name: Deck.swift
nodes.summary: Read-only Codable content model for a deck loaded from JSON, with entitlement gating, gender-dynamic card filtering, ordering, and ritual-presence helpers.

nodes.name: Deck
nodes.summary: Codable, Identifiable deck struct holding metadata and its cards; methods gate availability by AccessTier and filter/sort cards by GenderDynamic and sortOrder.

nodes.name: DeckProgress.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Model tracking a couple's resume position through a deck across sittings (current card index, unwrapped flag, first-opened/completed dates).

nodes.name: DeckProgress
nodes.summary: @Model keyed by coupleId + String deckId persisting mid-deck resume index and ceremonial-unwrap state; distinct from per-card CardResult.

nodes.name: DesireItem.swift
nodes.summary: Codable model for a single cohort-adaptive Desire Map prompt loaded from JSON, with per-track answer strings (weight-ordered) and cohort-appearance helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Only the answer weight (index) is ever stored/synced; the displayed string is cohort-specific copy.

nodes.name: DesireItem
nodes.summary: Codable, Identifiable, Hashable struct describing a desire prompt (category, sensitivity, tracks, per-track answers) with answers(for:) and appears(in:) lookups.

nodes.name: DesireMatch.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Model for a server-computed mutual/adjacent Desire Map match; reveal state and free-reveal flag are Edge-Function-authoritative to keep the paywall un-bypassable.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains no individual ratings — only confirmed mutual positives computed server-side.

nodes.name: DesireMatch
nodes.summary: @Model holding coupleId, itemId, match type, and reveal state; isFreeReveal/revealedAt are set only by the Edge Function, with bridgeCardId linking to a CompanionCard.

nodes.name: DesireRating.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Models for the app's most sensitive data: per-user private DesireMapEntry ratings (notForMe never syncs) and the couple-level DesireMapStatus completion/reveal tracker.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-layer enforcement (Swift filter + Edge Function + RLS) keeps notForMe ratings on-device and partner-inaccessible. Despite the file name, it defines DesireMapEntry and DesireMapStatus.

nodes.name: DesireMapEntry
nodes.summary: @Model storing one user's private rating for a desire item; userId never crosses to the partner and isSyncable excludes notForMe so the sacred boundary never leaves the device.

nodes.name: DesireMapStatus
nodes.summary: @Model tracking per-couple Desire Map completion booleans and reveal state (both readable by partners) plus an explicit waitingStateSince powering the 7-day timer.

nodes.name: EntitlementRecord.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftData @Models for purchase records: couple-level EntitlementRecord (Core unlock, founding-member, lifetime/expiry) and the permanent $7.99 ConnectionEntitlement.
nodes.languageNotes: Receipt validation is server-side via Edge Function; purchasedBy is support-only and never shown to either partner.

nodes.name: EntitlementRecord
nodes.summary: @Model recording a Core-unlock StoreKit purchase for a couple, with founding-member, active, and lifetime/expiry computed state.

nodes.name: ConnectionEntitlement
nodes.summary: @Model for a permanent $7.99 additional-connection purchase with no expiry, surviving relationship dissolution.

nodes.name: ThemeManager.swift

nodes.name: ThemeManager

nodes.name: ThemeModifiers.swift

nodes.name: ThemedCardModifier

nodes.name: VaylPrimitives.swift

nodes.languageNotes: Strict token layering: primitives are appearance-named UIColor constants, never referenced in views — semantic naming is reserved for AppColors.

nodes.name: VaylPrimitives
nodes.summary: Enum namespace of raw UIColor constants — spectrum anchors, ink neutrals, OB canvas darks, felt/table tints, wine text scale, and gold safety signals.

nodes.name: VaylApp.swift
nodes.summary: App entry point and composition root: constructs the shared AppState and injects it into the store graph, wires environment objects, and runs session/entitlement bootstrap on launch.

nodes.name: VaylApp
nodes.summary: @main App struct owning all top-level @State stores (ThemeManager, AppState, OnboardingStore, EntitlementStore, etc.); hydrates onboarding state, checks auth session, bootstraps entitlements, and retries pending syncs.

nodes.name: AppIconRetreival.swift

nodes.summary: Asset catalog manifest registering the custom 'gender-man' SF Symbol used in onboarding gender selection.

nodes.name: ensureProfileExists

nodes.name: settle
nodes.summary: Projects a target card from release velocity, clamps it to maxFlick and topology bounds, and snaps position; must be called inside withAnimation.

nodes.name: ThreeCardFanController.swift
nodes.summary: Three-card-monte fan controller orchestrating SpriteKit deal flights, spread-turnover reveal, lift, and confirm physics for the candle/experience-level selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges a SpriteKit CardFlightScene for the deal, then hands off to SwiftUI card backs; respects Reduce Motion.

nodes.name: ThreeMonteState
nodes.summary: Equatable state machine enum for the three-card fan lifecycle, carrying CandleIntensity in its lifted/confirming/done cases.

nodes.name: ThreeCardFanController
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor controller managing three-card fan positions, flips, z-ordering, and the deal/reveal/lift/confirm sequences.

nodes.name: deal
nodes.summary: Flies three card backs one at a time from the dealer point into fan slots via the SpriteKit flight scene with correct z-ordering.

nodes.name: spreadTurnoverReveal
nodes.summary: Opens the fan into a spread, sweep-turns the cards face-up left to right, then re-collects them into the resting fan.

nodes.name: lift
nodes.summary: Lifts the tapped card to a cinematic position while receding the other two cards.

nodes.name: confirm
nodes.summary: Pockets the chosen card to the corner deck, clears the non-selected cards, and fires onConfirm at landing.

nodes.name: runEntrance
nodes.summary: Runs the full entrance sequence (deal, SpriteKit-to-SwiftUI handoff, flourish, reveal).

nodes.name: CardRevealPillButton.swift
nodes.summary: Toggleable pill-shaped selection button for card reveal options, scaling when selected with a staggered entrance and dimming sibling pills.

nodes.name: CardRevealPillButton
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View rendering a single selectable reveal pill with spectrum border, scale, staggered entrance, and accessibility traits.

nodes.name: CardShadows.swift
nodes.summary: View extension providing a reusable dual-shadow card modifier (accent color wash plus a black depth shadow), theme-aware.

nodes.name: CardStyle.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable card-shell ViewModifier and convenience extension applying background, rounded clip, and border stroke to eliminate repetition.

nodes.name: CardStyle
nodes.summary: ViewModifier applying background fill, rounded-rectangle clip, and border stroke for a standard card shell.

nodes.name: CategoryTileView.swift
nodes.summary: Home-screen category grid tile displaying an emoji, title, completion count, and progress bar with theme-aware styling.

nodes.name: CategoryTileView
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View rendering a category tile with emoji icon, title, card-count label, and a completion progress bar.

nodes.name: ConversationCard.swift
nodes.summary: 3D-flipping conversation card with a question/fuse-timer front and a response pill-grid back, managing flip state, pill selection, encouragement, and ghost-deck atmosphere.

nodes.name: ConversationCard
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View for a flippable conversation card composing front and back faces, pill selection handling, and optional fuse timer and ghost deck.

nodes.name: highlightedQuestion
nodes.summary: Builds an AttributedString question with markdown highlighting applied to a specific target phrase.

nodes.name: handlePillSelection
nodes.summary: Records the selected pill, invokes onPillSelected, then schedules the encouragement display and onContinue callback on delays.

nodes.name: ConversationCardTypes.swift
nodes.summary: Model types backing the conversation card: OBCard onboarding data, pill/response enums, content discriminators, and fuse/ghost-deck configuration.

nodes.name: OBCard
nodes.summary: Model struct holding onboarding card data: overline, question, highlighted phrase, and back-face content.

nodes.name: CardRevealPill
nodes.summary: String-backed CaseIterable Identifiable enum of response pill labels (ready, figuring, scared, almostSaid, noApology).

nodes.name: ConversationCardContent
nodes.summary: Enum discriminating the conversation card content source (free prompt vs onboarding OBCard).

nodes.name: GhostDeckMode
nodes.summary: Enum configuring the background card-deck atmosphere (none, atmospheric, navigable).

nodes.name: CuriosityCardBack.swift
nodes.summary: Face-down side of the curiosity picker card rendering a laser-engraved maze texture with an embedded orbit animation that stops on flip.

nodes.name: CuriosityCardBack
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View drawing the curiosity card back: gradient base, ambient glow, maze pattern with orbit, corner marks, and spectrum border.

nodes.name: CuriosityFlipCard.swift
nodes.summary: Generic 3D flip container for curiosity picker cards: a maze/orbit back face and caller-supplied front content, orchestrating the flip and orbit lifecycle.

nodes.name: CuriosityFlipCard
nodes.summary: Generic SwiftUI View flipping between a CuriosityCardBack and arbitrary front content with a spring rotation3D animation.

nodes.name: FuseTimerView.swift
nodes.summary: Canvas-drawn fuse/spark countdown timer that travels a glowing spark along a rounded-rectangle border with a progressive burn effect.

nodes.name: FuseTimerView
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View rendering an animated fuse spark via Canvas with unburned-segment, ember, and multi-layer spark-head passes.

nodes.name: PremiumCardShell.swift
nodes.summary: Generic glass-morphic card container wrapping arbitrary content in an eight-layer premium composition with optional fuse-burn progress animation.

nodes.name: PremiumCardShell
nodes.summary: Generic SwiftUI View composing base fill, specular and internal reflections, ambient orbs, premium border, and an optional fuse spark over content.

nodes.name: HomeGlowField.swift
nodes.summary: Full-page atmospheric background for the Home dashboard that crossfades from drifting glow blobs (morphProgress 0) to a deep-space galaxy with stars and blooms (morphProgress 1) as the user scrolls.
nodes.languageNotes: Body is split into glowBlobLayer and galaxyLayer ViewBuilders so each type-checks in isolation (the inline two-layer ZStack with per-blob sin offsets compiled at 163ms).

nodes.name: HomeGlowField
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View that opacity-crossfades a seven-blob glow atmosphere against a galaxy layer driven by morphProgress, orchestrating staggered fade-in and looping drift animations on appear.

nodes.name: LightAuraBloom.swift
nodes.summary: Light-mode analogue of FlameAura: Canvas-drawn layered warm blobs (rose, peach, gold, lavender) that rise and breathe above a selected pill on cream backgrounds, scaled by the pill's intensity.

nodes.name: LightAuraBloom
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View rendering the intensity-scaled warm bloom; returns EmptyView for the .dim intensity and animates the blobs via a 60fps phase timer on a Canvas.

nodes.name: LightModeShimmer.swift
nodes.summary: Light-mode foil shimmer matching HolographicShimmer's energy on cream surfaces using two offset diagonal gradient sweeps in a warm purple/magenta/gold palette with screen blending.

nodes.name: LightModeShimmer
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View animating a primary horizontal sweep plus an optional offset secondary diagonal pass (pills only) so the two never sync, faking foil depth without strobing.

nodes.name: MazePatternView.swift
nodes.summary: Concentric engraved maze-ring pattern (rings with gaps plus radial spokes) rendered in multiple stroke layers for a debossed look, with a co-centered TileOrbitView comet orbit at its core.

nodes.name: MazePatternView
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View building the gapped-ring and spoke maze Path in four offset stroke layers (glow, groove shadow, main line, highlight) and embedding a TileOrbitView sharing its GeometryReader coordinate space.

nodes.name: OrbitSpark.metal
nodes.summary: Metal fragment shader that draws a single comet-like spark orbiting a rounded-rectangle border, using a signed-distance border mask and a time-driven angular sweep with mode-switchable dark/light spectrum colors.

nodes.name: orbitSpark
nodes.summary: Stitchable color shader computing a rounded-rect SDF border mask and a spark whose angular position orbits over time, tinted along a cyan/purple/magenta (or warm aurora) ramp with a hot white core.

nodes.name: OrbitSparkBorderView.swift
nodes.summary: SwiftUI wrapper that strokes a rounded-rectangle border with the spectrum gradient and applies the orbitSpark Metal colorEffect, animating the spark via TimelineView elapsed time and passing a palette mode for dark/light.

nodes.name: OrbitSparkBorderView
nodes.summary: View that drives the orbitSpark shader with size, elapsed time, border width, corner radius, and a color-mode float selecting the dark or warm palette.

nodes.name: PillBorder.swift
nodes.summary: View-extension modifiers (.pillBorder / .magentaGoldBorder) that add a crisp spectrum or warm gradient stroke plus a blurred outward glow duplicate to selected/active pill surfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: Public View extension delegates to a shared private PillBorderModifier so both the dark spectrum and light warm variants reuse one stroke-plus-glow implementation.

nodes.name: SectionHeader.swift
nodes.summary: Trivial all-caps muted section label used above sections in Settings, Home, and list screens.

nodes.name: SparkField.swift
nodes.summary: Campfire-ember particle system for light-mode onboarding screens: per-screen tuned configurations drive a Canvas of rising, drifting, life-cycled warm sparks composited below content.
nodes.languageNotes: Each SparkField owns an isolated @StateObject SparkSystem (no singleton); plusLighter blending is applied only inside the Canvas while .compositingGroup() seals the layer so sparks sit beneath ZStack siblings.

nodes.name: SparkConfiguration
nodes.summary: Value-type configuration capturing all per-screen spark tuning (count, speed, radius, glow, opacity ceilings, spawn and fade zones, palette), with one static preset per onboarding screen and a shared warm-ember default palette.

nodes.name: SparkField
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View driving its owned SparkSystem on a 30fps TimelineView Canvas, compositing-grouped and hit-testing-disabled so it always sits in the background.

nodes.name: SparkSystem
nodes.summary: ObservableObject owning the isolated particle array: configures, updates, and draws all particles with plusLighter blending inside the Canvas context.

nodes.name: Particle
nodes.summary: Value-type ember spark storing normalized position, velocity, drift, lifecycle frames, and color; computes a combined lifecycle-plus-spatial opacity curve and draws a three-layer halo/dot/core, respawning when aged out.

nodes.name: SpectrumHairline.swift
nodes.summary: Shared spectrum hairline accents: a flat fading cyan-purple-magenta line and a tapered lens/spindle variant (thickest at center, sharp points at the ends) used as on-brand sheet edge accents.

nodes.name: SpectrumHairline
nodes.summary: View rendering a thin full-width spectrum gradient line that fades to clear at both ends, the app's hairline accent shared by on-brand sheets.

nodes.name: TaperedSpectrumHairline
nodes.summary: View filling a lens-shaped tapered hairline with the spectrum gradient, mirroring VaylBorderEffect's tapered hairline at section scale for the OB sheet top-edge accent.

nodes.name: TileOrbitView.swift
nodes.summary: Purpose-built comet-orbit animation for small tile contexts (44-88pt): resting static arcs or TimelineView-driven comet orbits whose phase offsets and color cycling keep heads and hues from ever colliding.
nodes.languageNotes: Canvas-only with no state objects or trail history; resting state mounts no TimelineView so it costs zero GPU, and color cycling lerps the whole orbit as a unit through cyan/magenta/purple anchors.

nodes.name: TileOrbitView
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View switching between a resting-arc Canvas and an active comet Canvas; the active draw renders dim rings, a segmented gradient tail, a radial head glow, and a white-hot dot per orbit.

nodes.name: VaylBorderEffect.swift
nodes.summary: The animated spectrum pill border for VaylButton: a tapered hairline at rest that fills with a two-sided crisp-plus-halo spectrum stroke on press, with an outward-only glow achieved via destinationOut masking.
nodes.languageNotes: Stroke width is linearly interpolated across resting/active/glowing states to avoid a single-frame snap; the halo is masked inward (positive inset) so only the outward blur survives, and hairline visibility is decoupled from progress to close a one-frame gap.

nodes.name: VaylBorderEffect
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View composing the hairline, resting boundary stroke, masked outward halo, and two trimmed crisp spectrum strokes whose width, blur, and opacity are driven by progress and glowIntensity.

nodes.name: VaylButton.swift
nodes.summary: The app's primary CTA button: a holographic-shimmer pill with the animated VaylBorderEffect, choreographing press-down haptics, border fill, and a precisely scheduled post-fill glow burst on release.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CACurrentMediaTime (monotonic) rather than Date() to schedule the glow so NTP drift cannot mis-fire it; a custom DragGesture with ±20pt slop replaces a tap to get accurate inside/cancel detection at the 44pt hit target.

nodes.name: VaylButton
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View stacking the capsule fill, HolographicShimmer, VaylBorderEffect, and label, with gesture handlers that drive press state, haptics, border progress, and the async glow task.

nodes.name: onPressUp
nodes.summary: Release handler that fires medium haptic, computes remaining border-fill time from the monotonic press timestamp, then runs an async task sequencing glow-in, hold, and glow-out before invoking the action.

nodes.name: VaylFlourishView.swift
nodes.summary: Signature decorative flourish: two gradient-stroked paths that orbit and meet at a single center node, encoding duality and connection, breathing gently via an ambient pulse animation.

nodes.name: VaylFlourishView
nodes.summary: Canvas-based SwiftUI View drawing the mirrored flourish arms, curls, tails, and secondary waves with per-stroke linear gradients plus a small center node, scaled by an ambient breathing animation.

nodes.name: InteractiveField.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable icon-plus-TextField input row with a color-scheme-adaptive background, subtle border, and a soft accent shadow in dark mode.

nodes.name: ToggleRow.swift
nodes.summary: Trivial icon-plus-label-plus-Toggle row used in Settings sections, bound to an external isOn state.

nodes.name: FoilDeckTheme.swift
nodes.summary: Pure data model defining a deck's visual identity for the FoilOpen sealed-case ceremony: a three-stop colorway ramp plus the deck name embossed on the case front.
nodes.languageNotes: Two Equatable value-type structs with static factory constants (.solo, .vayl) — the Model layer of the 4-layer architecture.

nodes.name: FoilColorway
nodes.summary: Ordered three-stop color ramp (c0/c1/c2) expressing a deck category's identity; the .solo preset maps to the app's cyan-purple-magenta spectrum.

nodes.name: FoilDeckTheme
nodes.summary: Deck theme value type pairing a FoilColorway with a deck name; the .vayl static is the onboarding starter deck identity.

nodes.name: MetallicCaseView.swift
nodes.summary: The FoilOpen ceremony's reusable 3D tuck-box: a Canvas projects a painter-sorted anodized-metal box whose front face carries a debossed hex lattice lit by the hexFoilSurface Metal shader, then choreographs rise, knock, tap-to-crack, tear propagation, and shatter-dissolve.
nodes.languageNotes: 1400-line single View doing manual 3D projection, convex-hull silhouette clipping, inverse-bilinear face-space UV mapping, and per-frame shader uniform synthesis; band phase is driven by float tilt (never absolute time) to dodge float32 GPU precision loss.

nodes.name: CaseTear
nodes.summary: Identifiable model for a crack in the sealed case, anchored in face-local UV with a seed, strike timestamp, and fracture orientation so the tear sticks to the case through float and tilt.

nodes.name: MetallicCaseView
nodes.summary: Content-agnostic SwiftUI View rendering the floating metallic deck case; exposes a large tunable surface plus ceremony hooks (riseStart, latticeWakeStart, tears, dissolveStart, onFaceTap, knockStart, coreGlow) driven entirely by injected dates.

nodes.name: caseGeometry
nodes.summary: Computes the per-frame CaseGeometry — tilt radians, hue driver, pose-mixed box fit, and the 8 projected corners plus front quad — so the Canvas box and shader front-quad uniforms always agree.

nodes.name: foilLayer
nodes.summary: Assembles the layered ceremony render: interior glow behind erased wounds, the case Canvas with the hexFoilSurface colorEffect shader, and the shatter shard Canvas during the flood phase.

nodes.name: drawCase
nodes.summary: Draws the 3D box into the Canvas: culls and painter-sorts visible faces, fills each with single-light per-face brightness for the 3D read, adds the edge catch-light rim, and stamps the embossed brand layer.

nodes.name: drawBrand
nodes.summary: Renders the perspective-correct hairline frame and the embossed deck-name wordmark on the front face using bilinear quad mapping and the card-back emboss recipe (shadow, highlight, gradient-filled core passes).

nodes.name: drawTears
nodes.summary: Renders cracks in face space, mapping each tear's branch polylines through the current projected quad every frame with authored 1-2-3 severity, propagation growth, impact flash, and escalating colorway light-bleed.

nodes.name: handleTap
nodes.summary: Converts a view-space tap to face-local UV via inverse-bilinear of the current projected front quad, clamps it onto the face, and forwards it to the consumer's onFaceTap callback.

nodes.name: SpectrumSparkField.swift
nodes.summary: Stateless spectrum-mote particle field for the FoilOpen ceremony: TimelineView-driven, seeded-deterministic spark bursts knocked loose by a strike on the case, popping outward then drifting up like embers.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-function-of-time Canvas rendering with a SplitMix-style seeded RandomNumberGenerator so particle params are recomputed identically every frame — no mutable particle state to clean up.

nodes.name: SparkBurst
nodes.summary: Identifiable value type describing one strike's worth of sparks — origin, start time, seed, count, and strike-vs-burst style — with a static lifespan used as the safe pruning horizon.

nodes.name: SpectrumSparkField
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View that draws all live spark bursts on a TimelineView Canvas with two-pass glow-plus-core rendering; suppresses all airborne particles under Reduce Motion.

nodes.name: GlowOrb.swift
nodes.summary: Small reusable radial-gradient glow orb whose ambient opacity adapts to the color scheme (emissive in dark, barely-there in light); decorative and non-interactive.

nodes.name: GlowUnderline.swift
nodes.summary: ViewModifier that overlays a breathing three-layer spectrum glow underline (solid core, tight neon, wide bloom) beneath its content, with a flash parameter to dim it on interaction.

nodes.name: GlowUnderlineView.swift
nodes.summary: Standalone View variant of the breathing spectrum glow underline (solid core, neon glow, wide bloom) for cases that compose the underline directly rather than as a modifier.

nodes.name: GradBadge.swift
nodes.summary: Small capsule pill with a spectrum gradient border and a text label, used for metadata counts, status tags, and category chips — never for CTAs.

nodes.name: HolographicShimmer.metal
nodes.summary: Metal shader library of stitchable fragment effects: oil-slick orb iridescence, Voronoi and folded-simplex caustics, specular sweeps, a holographic foil surface, and the debossed hex-lattice foil that fronts the MetallicCaseView tuck-box.
nodes.languageNotes: Shared noise toolkit (value-noise fbm, simplex fbmS, Voronoi F1/F2, hashes) reused across seven [[stitchable]] entry points; hexFoilSurface uses inverse-bilinear to map screen pixels into the projected front-face UV so the lattice foreshortens with tilt and terminates at the box fold.

nodes.name: holoColor
nodes.summary: Stitchable color shader compositing the oil-slick orb field with an ink overlay and animated surface grain, producing the pill-background holographic wash.

nodes.name: holoDistort
nodes.summary: Stitchable distortion shader perturbing sample positions with two fbm noise fields for a subtle liquid warp of the holographic surface.

nodes.name: causticLayer
nodes.summary: Stitchable shader rendering an animated light-web from two sine-warped Voronoi layers combined on their F2-F1 boundary, with vignette and an indigo-to-lavender color ramp for the OB deep card face.

nodes.name: htmlCaustics
nodes.summary: Stitchable shader porting the reference HTML caustics: folded simplex fBm with a sinkhole distortion produces sharp bright light ridges over a deep-purple-to-lavender ramp.

nodes.name: holoSpecular
nodes.summary: Stitchable shader adding a moving white specular highlight band (plus a secondary glint) to brighten the holographic surface where light catches it.

nodes.name: holoFoilSurface
nodes.summary: Stitchable foil shader producing electric high-contrast spectrum specular bands with an oil-slick or liquid-chrome pattern, a moving highlight, controlled gloss, and sparse sparkle over a dark base.

nodes.name: hexFoilSurface
nodes.summary: Stitchable face-space material that maps pixels into the projected front quad via inverse-bilinear and lights a debossed hex lattice: groove flanks ignite in the deck colorway as one tilt-phased anisotropic band sweeps, with anodized grain, fresnel rim, environment gradient, and core glow.

nodes.name: HolographicShimmer.swift
nodes.summary: Pure-SwiftUI (Canvas, no Metal) holographic pill background: a noise-driven specular sweep, five screen-blended drifting orbs, vignette, and two fbm grain layers, used as the VaylButton fill.
nodes.languageNotes: Despite the shared name, this is a CPU/Canvas effect distinct from HolographicShimmer.metal; the grain is precomputed into a tiled CGImage and value-noise drives the sweep so the per-frame TimelineView closure stays cheap to type-check.

nodes.name: HolographicShimmer
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View that layers the base fill, noise-swept specular gradient, drifting screen-blend orbs, dim overlay, vignette, and coarse/fine grain into the animated holographic pill surface.

nodes.name: GrainCanvas
nodes.summary: Private helper View that builds one fbm-noise grain tile as a CGImage and tiles it across the Canvas, extracted so the drawing closure stays trivial to type-check.

nodes.name: NavArrow.swift
nodes.summary: Adaptive onboarding back/forward nav-arrow component family: a custom chevron Shape rendered as a spectrum-gradient stroked pill, with dark and light variants unified behind a colorScheme-aware OnboardingNavArrow wrapper.
nodes.languageNotes: Param ordering (size before action) is deliberate to enable trailing-closure call sites; body decomposed into computed sub-views to keep Swift type-checking fast.

nodes.name: NavArrowShape
nodes.summary: SwiftUI Shape that draws a double-line chevron glyph (ported from an HTML SVG viewBox) with all points proportional to the rect.

nodes.name: GradientStrokeArrow
nodes.summary: Renders NavArrowShape stroked with a configurable gradient plus two layered glow shadows.

nodes.name: DarkNavArrow
nodes.summary: Dark-mode nav arrow: a modal-background pill with a spectrum pillBorder, blurred glow duplicate, and centered spectrum arrow glyph.

nodes.name: LightNavArrow
nodes.summary: Light-mode nav arrow: card-background pill with aurora or magenta-gold border and a warm magenta-gold arrow glyph.

nodes.name: OnboardingNavArrow
nodes.summary: Adaptive drop-in wrapper selecting the dark or light nav arrow by colorScheme and mirroring horizontally for forward direction; the single onboarding navigation entry point.

nodes.name: OBSheetChrome.swift
nodes.summary: Shared ViewModifier giving the onboarding custom sheets native-style chrome: full-bleed dark surface with continuous rounded top corners and a spectrum border that traces the top edge and dissolves down the sides.
nodes.languageNotes: Exposed via an `obSheetChrome(widen:)` View extension; uses layered LinearGradient masks to taper the spectrum border off both axes.

nodes.name: OnboardingFooter.swift
nodes.summary: Small footer label shown beneath the CTA on onboarding screens, carrying privacy/encryption reassurance copy at minimum-legible Dynamic Type size.

nodes.name: OnboardingNavBar.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable onboarding top row pairing an optional circular back button with a centered OnboardingProgressBar; used at the head of every onboarding screen showing navigation.

nodes.name: RacetrackTabBar.swift
nodes.summary: Main app tab bar binding to AppTab: a capsule of icon pills with a sequenced racetrack-trim border animation (old pill erases while new pill draws) over a shimmering glass background.
nodes.languageNotes: Bar owns all per-tab trim values so it can coordinate the reverse-then-forward handoff without pills fighting; animation guarded by an isAnimating lock.

nodes.name: RacetrackTabBar
nodes.summary: Tab bar view that binds the selected AppTab and runs the coordinated draw/erase border sequence on tab change.

nodes.name: SafariView.swift
nodes.summary: Thin UIViewControllerRepresentable wrapper over SFSafariViewController for presenting hosted legal web pages in-app via a sheet.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges UIKit's SFSafariViewController into SwiftUI; iOS 26 compliant (WKWebView-backed Safari controller, not the banned UIWebView).

nodes.name: TabContentWrapper.swift
nodes.summary: Generic wrapper applied to every tab's content that adds bottom scroll inset, a gradient fade mask, and indicator inset so content dissolves cleanly behind the floating tab bar.

nodes.name: OnboardingProgressBar.swift
nodes.summary: Heavily-tuned onboarding progress bar with a gradient fill, traveling shimmer, and a multi-layer Canvas bloom + particle completion effect; ships extracted pure-math helpers, a testable animation clock, and full accessibility/RTL/localization support.
nodes.languageNotes: Animation math and clock are factored out of the View into a pure enum and an @Observable class for unit-testability; a 30fps TimelineView drives the bloom and is paused on background.

nodes.name: AnimationMath
nodes.summary: UI-free namespace of pure functions computing shimmer phase, bloom/breathe intensity, shimmer X offset, and a NaN-safe progress ratio.

nodes.name: ProgressAnimationClock
nodes.summary: @Observable clock tracking an activation timestamp and computing elapsed seconds, extracted so the bar's animation state is testable and identity-safe.

nodes.name: OnboardingProgressBar
nodes.summary: The progress bar View itself: derives fill geometry, branches dark/light/RTL gradients, and composes the static bar plus the TimelineView-driven bloom canvas and particle system on completion.

nodes.name: OrbitIndicator.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable three-state (pending → processing → complete) loading indicator drawing an animated comet-tail orbit via a TimelineView Canvas, with a gradient fill, glow, and holographic sheen for the complete state.
nodes.languageNotes: Spark head uses .color shading rather than radialGradient because the latter is silently dropped by Xcode's preview canvas renderer.

nodes.name: OrbitIndicator
nodes.summary: Public three-state orbit indicator View that layers a pending ring, animated orbit canvas, complete gradient fill, glow, and a sweeping holographic sheen.

nodes.name: OrbitIndicatorState
nodes.summary: Equatable enum of the indicator's lifecycle: pending (static ring), processing (animated orbit), complete (filled + glow).

nodes.name: ProgressBar.swift
nodes.summary: Minimal value/max horizontal capsule progress bar filled with the spectrum border gradient.

nodes.name: ProgressRingView.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable circular progress ring with a spectrum-stroked arc and a centered percentage label, sized and weighted by init parameters.

nodes.name: ScoreRing.swift
nodes.summary: Animated score ring rendering a 0-100 score as an angular spectrum sweep with a centered numeric label that fills in on appear.

nodes.name: ScreenshotProtectionModifier.swift
nodes.summary: ViewModifier that obscures private content with a material overlay when the screen is being captured or screenshotted, exposed via a screenshotProtected() View extension.
nodes.languageNotes: Observes UIApplication.userDidTakeScreenshot and UIScreen.capturedDidChange notifications; resolves the active window scene rather than using the banned UIScreen.main.

nodes.name: SpectrumBar.swift
nodes.summary: Trivial horizontal spectrum-gradient capsule used as a thin divider/accent bar.

nodes.name: GradientText.swift
nodes.summary: Static two-tone gradient text view (no animation), colorScheme-aware; the non-animated counterpart to LivingText's premium three-tone treatment.

nodes.name: KeywordHighlightText.swift
nodes.summary: Text view that builds an AttributedString highlighting specified keywords in accent colors (cyan/magenta/gold) against a base color for prompt emphasis.

nodes.name: LivingText.swift
nodes.summary: Premium animated tri-color 'breathing' headline text: a TimelineView layers screen-blended bloom glows and a subtle scale breath over a full-opacity spectrum core, with a static Reduce Motion fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-frame math is moved into an explicitly-typed Frame struct resolved in init so the TimelineView closure stays cheap to type-check (was ~216ms inline).

nodes.name: LivingText
nodes.summary: Animated breathing-gradient headline View with three screen-blended Text layers (outer bloom, inner glow, crisp core) driven by a TimelineView.

nodes.name: SpectrumBulletRow.swift
nodes.summary: Labeled list row marked by a flat spectrum disc with a periodic holographic specular sweep; intentionally not a checkmark so lists read as possibilities rather than guaranteed deliverables.
nodes.languageNotes: phaseOffset (by index) staggers the sweep so a list cascades rather than flashing in lockstep; sweep gated off under Reduce Motion.

nodes.name: SignInView.swift
nodes.summary: Sign-in screen presenting the Vayl wordmark, a Sign in with Apple CTA wired to AuthService, loading/error states, and tappable Terms/Privacy links that open in an in-app SafariView sheet.
nodes.languageNotes: Intercepts custom vayl-legal:// markdown links via an OpenURLAction so legal links open the SafariView sheet instead of leaving the app.

nodes.name: SignInView
nodes.summary: Auth entry View that forwards Apple sign-in to its injected AuthService and reflects the service's loading/error state.

nodes.name: CompanionCardStore.swift
nodes.summary: Stub @Observable @MainActor Store that maps a couple's DesireMatches to companion cards pointing at a conversation or deck to open together; returns placeholder content until real companion data and deck mapping are wired.

nodes.name: CompanionCardStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor store producing placeholder CompanionCards from a list of DesireMatches.

nodes.name: DesireMapStore.swift
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Store for the Desire Map rater: resolves the cohort track from the local SwiftData profile, loads track items, upserts one DesireMapEntry per (userId, itemId), marks the profile complete, and best-effort syncs on completion.
nodes.languageNotes: Local-first: completion flag is written to SwiftData independently of remote sync; uses #Predicate FetchDescriptors and a UserDefaults pending-sync retry flag.

nodes.name: DesireMapStore
nodes.summary: Rater store owning items, ratings, track, and load/rate/sync logic backed by SwiftData and ContentLoader.

nodes.name: DesireRevealStore.swift
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Store for the D4 reveal: fetches the couple's alignment-only matches via DesireSyncService and resolves the free/locked split against the EntitlementStore (the conversion gate).
nodes.languageNotes: Read path is privacy-scoped (alignment + is_free_reveal only, never raw partner answers); defines a Phase enum and a RevealMatch view-model struct; unlockAll() runs the Core purchase.

nodes.name: DesireRevealStore
nodes.summary: Reveal store that loads matches and computes unlocked/locked partitions based on Core entitlement.

nodes.name: RevealMatch

nodes.name: EntitlementStore.swift
nodes.summary: Monetization Store: the central @Observable read surface for the couple's access tier (isCore/tier), OR-ing the couple's server tier with local StoreKit ownership, and driving purchase/restore and background transaction updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Couple-level entitlement: one purchase unlocks both partners; Views read isCore and call purchase()/restore() while StoreKitService + EntitlementService do the I/O.

nodes.name: EntitlementStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Store resolving Core entitlement from server + StoreKit, exposing isCore/corePriceText and running purchase()/restore()/bootstrap().

nodes.name: bootstrap
nodes.summary: App-launch entry: loads the Core product, resolves the tier, and starts the StoreKit transaction-updates listener.

nodes.name: refresh
nodes.summary: Re-resolves isCore from local StoreKit ownership and the couple's server tier, keeping the last tier on a network blip.

nodes.name: purchase
nodes.summary: Runs the Core purchase, unlocks the buyer locally, pushes the signed JWS to the server so the partner unlocks too, then re-resolves.

nodes.name: restore
nodes.summary: Explicit Restore Purchases path: re-syncs from the App Store, re-grants the couple server-side if Core is owned, then re-resolves.

nodes.name: PaywallSheet.swift
nodes.summary: The reusable Vayl paywall: one Core-scoped bottom sheet ($24.99 one-time, both unlock) presented from three doors (reveal / settings / play-deck) whose only difference is the hook header; purchase and restore run through EntitlementStore.
nodes.languageNotes: ViewThatFits content-height with a scroll backstop for large Dynamic Type; legal links open an in-app Safari sheet.

nodes.name: PaywallSheet
nodes.summary: SwiftUI View for the paywall sheet, composing hook header, value bullets, price row, CTA, and legal footer; calls EntitlementStore.purchase()/restore().

nodes.name: purchase
nodes.summary: Kicks off the Core purchase via EntitlementStore and invokes onUnlocked when it succeeds.

nodes.name: restorePurchases
nodes.summary: Runs EntitlementStore.restore(), opening the gate on success or showing a 'nothing to restore' alert.

nodes.name: CardFlightEngine.swift

nodes.languageNotes: @MainActor class holding a weak VaylDirector; uses ImageRenderer to snapshot VaylCardBack and async/await over the scene's rest callback.

nodes.name: CardFlightEngine
nodes.summary: @MainActor engine that claims landing slots and deals single cards through the director's CardFlightScene, resolving rested positions via a checked continuation.

nodes.name: dealSingleCard
nodes.summary: Snapshots a VaylCardBack, picks a far-enough landing slot, decides overshoot, and flies the card via SpriteKit, returning its resting offset/angle/flightID.

nodes.name: sailCard
nodes.summary: Flies a single card image through the SpriteKit scene between two points and awaits its rested position and rotation.

nodes.name: DesireMapIndicator.swift

nodes.name: GravLiftView

nodes.name: HomeWidgetShell

nodes.name: OrbLayer

nodes.name: darkSurface
nodes.summary: Builds the dark-mode widget surface fill and layered gradients for the widget shell.

nodes.name: lightSurface
nodes.summary: Builds the light-mode widget surface fill and layered gradients for the widget shell.

nodes.name: rim
nodes.summary: Computes the spectrum rim overlay stroke that traces the widget edge.

nodes.name: PartnerChip.swift

nodes.summary: SwiftUI View switching on ReflectionCardState to render the appropriate reflection card; each state is a dedicated builder function for type-check performance.

nodes.name: ResearchTicker

nodes.name: GettingStarted.swift

nodes.languageNotes: No SwiftUI; the displayed Path and entry card both read this derived value, which is never stored.

nodes.name: GettingStarted
nodes.summary: Equatable value type holding the ordered onboarding steps with progress/nextStep helpers and a static resolve() that derives state from completion flags.

nodes.name: resolve
nodes.summary: Static factory that derives the GettingStarted activation (exactly one .active step, reveal gated on both maps) from myMapComplete/isPaired/partnerMapComplete/revealDone.

nodes.name: HomeEventEngine.swift
nodes.summary: Pure logic struct that selects the home greeting's two-line sub-copy from a prioritized list of HomeEvents (partner events → milestones → time/absence → stage defaults).

nodes.name: HomeEventEngine
nodes.summary: Stateless struct whose single static oneLiner() maps app/event state to one localized greeting string by priority.

nodes.name: oneLiner
nodes.summary: Static function returning the greeting sub-copy by walking events in priority order, then falling back to stage-based defaults.

nodes.name: HomeModels.swift
nodes.summary: View-layer Model definitions for the Home screen: the ReflectionCardState and HomeState routing enums, reflection pill groups, pick-up items, research facts, and the HomeEvent enum.
nodes.languageNotes: No business logic and no SwiftData; pure structs/enums consumed by the Home views and HomeStore.

nodes.name: HomeState
