nodes.name: ReflectionCardState
nodes.summary: Enum modeling the reflection card's lifecycle states (hidden / pendingYours / waitingOnPartner / bothReflected / summary) with their associated payloads.

nodes.name: HomeStore.swift
nodes.summary: The Home feature's Store: an @Observable @MainActor brain that owns all routing state, derives HomeState/GettingStarted, loads profile/desire-status/deck-progress/reflection/deck data, and tracks the one-shot map-completion beat.
nodes.languageNotes: Dependencies injected via init; ModelContext created fresh at write time; reads SwiftData (UserProfile/DeckProgress/CardSession) and remote DesireSyncService.

nodes.name: HomeStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Store deciding all Home routing and loading; the view renders, the store decides.

nodes.name: resolveHomeState
nodes.summary: Derives the current HomeState from completion + link flags; Home always leads with the dashboard, soloUnpaired only when solo and unlinked.

nodes.name: loadAll
nodes.summary: Async entry point that loads profile, desire status, deck progress, reflection state, and the deck in one pass on appear.

nodes.name: loadProfile
nodes.summary: Reads the local UserProfile to resolve map completion and the derived DesireMapState.

nodes.name: loadReflectionState
nodes.summary: Reads the most recent completed CardSession to derive the reflection card state (pendingYours when a finished session exists).

nodes.name: loadDeck
nodes.summary: Loads 'the-opener' deck via ContentLoader, publishing loading/error state for the dashboard.

nodes.name: CanvasEasing.swift
nodes.summary: Pure easing-math namespace (normalise-in-window, cubic in-out, quint/sept ease-out) used to drive the OB canvas dissolution and physics curves.
nodes.languageNotes: A caseless Swift enum used as a static-only function namespace — a common Swift idiom for stateless utilities.

nodes.name: CanvasEasing
nodes.summary: Static easing namespace: nm (window normalise + clamp), eIO3 (ease-in-out cubic), eO5/eO7 (ease-out quint/sept). The math backbone for GenderSequencer's dissolution curves.

nodes.name: OBDeepCardFace.swift
nodes.summary: Canvas + Metal-shader renderer for 'The Deep' OB card face revealed on flip during NamePhase: layered swirl, seeded particles, shimmer, depth-glow, and a caustics colorEffect.
nodes.languageNotes: Time-stateless renderer: deepT is injected by a parent TimelineView, and seeded particle/fleck pools are generated once via a deterministic sin-hash rng so frames stay stable.

nodes.name: OBDeepCardFace
nodes.summary: The Deep card-face View: composites a Canvas (base/swirl/shimmer/depth-glow) under a screen-blended htmlCaustics shader, clipped to the OB card radius with a spectrum DeepCardShell overlay.

nodes.name: OnboardingCanvasView.swift
nodes.summary: The single persistent OB canvas: a 10-layer ZStack (void → atmosphere → table → dealpoint → SpriteKit flight → cards → projected text → corner deck → phase overlay) plus the phase router switching on director.phase.
nodes.languageNotes: Hosts a SpriteView for card-flight physics and routes every OB phase via a switch on director.phase; modal overlays live in OnboardingCanvasWrapper outside the .ignoresSafeArea chain.

nodes.name: OnboardingCanvasView
nodes.summary: Root OB canvas View owning a VaylDirector; renders all persistent canvas layers and the phase overlay, and starts the director on appear.

nodes.name: OnboardingCanvasWrapper
nodes.summary: Call-site wrapper that captures the real safe-area insets outside the canvas's ignoresSafeArea chain and hosts the credential-editor and single-greeting overlays (which the canvas forbids as sheets).

nodes.name: PhaseOverlayView
nodes.summary: Private phase router: switches on director.phase to the active phase View and applies a depth cross-fade handoff (Reduce-Motion → pure opacity).

nodes.name: OnboardingStage.swift
nodes.summary: Narrow @MainActor protocol exposing only the coordinator surface (onboardingData + dealer-line projection) that extracted phase sequencers need, keeping them decoupled from the full director.
nodes.languageNotes: A deliberately minimal protocol seam: sequencers depend on OnboardingStage, not the 850-line VaylDirector, so they stay unit-testable against a mock stage.

nodes.name: OnboardingStage
nodes.summary: @MainActor AnyObject protocol: onboardingData accessor plus showDealerLineManual/hideDealerLine — the capability slice VaylDirector exposes to sequencers.

nodes.name: GenderSequencer.swift
nodes.summary: Extracted @Observable store owning the entire Gender phase: dissolution/crystallisation curves, radio-tuner signal, gender + pronouns drums, and tap-lift→swipe-up confirm, writing the choice back through OnboardingStage.
nodes.languageNotes: Holds an unowned OnboardingStage; one dissolutionT write per frame drives eight computed easing curves to keep SwiftUI invalidation minimal.

nodes.name: GenderSequencer
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Gender-phase store: runs the autonomous crystallise→flip→picker sequence, manages both drums + decline, and pockets the confirmed identity card while persisting genderA/pronounsA via the stage.

nodes.name: runRise
nodes.summary: Async crystallisation sequence: drives dissolutionT 0→1 over 3.2s with the dealer line firing mid-formation, then holds and hands off to the flip-and-spin beat. Includes a full Reduce-Motion instant path.

nodes.name: confirmSelection
nodes.summary: Swipe-up commit: persists self gender/pronouns into onboardingData (or nil on decline), flies the card to the corner deck on a pocket curve with late alpha, and sets shouldPocket for GenderPhase to advance.

nodes.name: TableSurfaceView.swift
nodes.summary: Animatable single-pass Canvas that draws the entire Vayl card table (atmosphere, felt, vignette, topo contours with card dissolution warp/flow-around + forge sway, compass star, amber pool, spectrum rim arc).
nodes.languageNotes: Conforms to Animatable so fade/rimBurst/forgeEnergy interpolate per-frame under the caller's withAnimation; visibility is purely fade-driven, never conditional. Includes a rounded-rect SDF for topo flow-around.

nodes.name: TableSurfaceView
nodes.summary: Layer-3 table-surface View (View + Animatable). Caller drives fade/rimBurst/dissolution/forgeEnergy; the body composites seven Canvas sub-passes and never holds state or animates itself.

nodes.name: drawTopoLines
nodes.summary: Draws 62 fbm/domain-warped contour lines clipped to the table circle, applying forge sway plus a two-phase card dissolution (inward warp, then rounded-rect SDF flow-around) when a gender card is crystallising.

nodes.name: drawSpectrumRim
nodes.summary: Renders the tapered cyan→purple→magenta rim arc in 120 segments with a base-bloom + crisp pass, amplified by rimBurst on card impact and spotlit at the compass-star midpoint.

nodes.name: drawCompassStar
nodes.summary: Draws the 8-spike compass star at the deal point with glow, halo/inner rings, per-spike light/shadow faces, and a filled center octagon.

nodes.name: VaylDirector.swift
nodes.summary: The central @Observable @MainActor OB coordinator: owns phase, all card collections + physics state, table fade, and the only advance() path; conforms to OnboardingStage and forwards to the projector, card-flight engine, and per-phase sequencers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @ObservationIgnored lazy vars to defer SpriteKit/engine/sequencer construction; advance() debounces with a 50ms Task and is the sole writer of director.phase and tableFade per the architecture contract.

nodes.name: VaylDirector
nodes.summary: OB flow coordinator (Store layer) implementing OnboardingStage: drives phase entry routines, credential collection, context/curiosity conclusions, opener-deck evaluation, and the final commit through OnboardingStore.

nodes.name: advance
nodes.summary: The sole phase-transition gate: debounces re-entry, hides the outgoing dealer line, then sets phase and runs the new phase's entry routine on a short delayed Task.

nodes.name: concludeContext
nodes.summary: Writes the chosen relationship context + register, re-emerges the felt with a responsive dealer reply, credits the .context credential, then advances to Curiosity after the copy beat.

nodes.name: finishOnboarding
nodes.summary: Commits onboardingData through OnboardingStore (gated by isReadyToComplete); on success dissolves the felt as reactive routing exits OB, on failure sets commitFailed non-destructively.

nodes.name: evaluateOpenerDeckType
nodes.summary: Selects the OpenerDeckType from NMStage + situational register + curiosity richness (mode-independent by design) and writes it into onboardingData at the end of Curiosity.

nodes.name: receiveCredential
nodes.summary: The single path for crediting the corner deck: appends a freshly collected credential card and plays the deck-receive pulse so card-state ownership stays in the director.

nodes.name: CornerDeckView.swift
nodes.summary: Stateless top-right corner deck showing collected credential cards (max 7) as a stacked, slightly fanned pile of real VaylCardBack renders with a count label and receive-glow.

nodes.name: CornerDeckView
nodes.summary: Renders the corner credential pile from [VaylCardModel] with hardcoded stacking offsets and a derived scale so each mini-card's corner radius matches the cornerCard token.

nodes.name: DeckWrapView.swift
nodes.summary: TimelineView-driven Canvas that spirals spectrum ribbons in a spinning helix around the rising BuildDeck 'forge' deck, drawing front strands bright and back strands dim.

nodes.name: DeckWrapView
nodes.summary: Forge-beat helix View: draws three phase-offset spectrum ribbons around the deck centre via a Canvas, with intensity fading the wrap in as the deck builds.

nodes.name: FounderLetterSheet.swift
nodes.summary: Shared generic sheet chrome (spectrum grab handle + obSheetChrome) for the founder letter, used identically at BuildDeck's peek detent and FounderLetterPhase's full expansion so the phase swap is invisible.

nodes.name: FounderLetterSheet
nodes.summary: Generic View<Content> rendering the founder-letter sheet frame: spectrum capsule handle over @ViewBuilder content with native obSheetChrome.

nodes.name: HandBackFollow.swift
nodes.summary: Pure feel-math namespace for the shared 'lift → swipe up to hand back' affordance: tanh-banded lateral drift, stiffened upward follow with a soft ceiling, and a slight drift tilt — tuned from one place.
nodes.languageNotes: Static-only enum applying tanh soft-clamps so a hard flick can't launch the card off-screen; drives visual follow only, with commit keyed off raw translation at the call site.

nodes.name: HandBackFollow
nodes.summary: Static offset/tilt helpers for a lifted hand-back card: bounded tanh drift and stiffened upward lift, plus a drift-proportional tilt in degrees.

nodes.name: LiftHalo.swift
nodes.summary: Single-source spectrum focus ring shown around a lifted OB card (blurred halo + crisp glowing stroke), reused as an overlay by every selection phase so the tap-to-lift look never drifts.

nodes.name: LiftHalo
nodes.summary: Hit-test-disabled View rendering the lifted-card spectrum ring in two passes with spectrumBorderGlow, animated on a `visible` flag.

nodes.name: OnboardingAtmosphere.swift
nodes.summary: Per-phase OB atmosphere: light mode composites AuroraGlowField + SparkField, dark mode renders an OBVoidBloom (tri-color bloom under a hard void mask), with a library of named AtmosphereConfig presets.
nodes.languageNotes: AtmosphereConfig/AtmosphereIntensity are Equatable value presets so per-phase config changes can be animated via atmosphereShift.

nodes.name: OnboardingAtmosphere
nodes.summary: Atmosphere View that branches on colorScheme: aurora + sparks in light, OBVoidBloom in dark, mapping the active AtmosphereConfig into an AuroraConfig.

nodes.name: AtmosphereConfig
nodes.summary: Equatable preset bundle pairing light/dark AtmosphereIntensity values, with named statics (stat, brand, name, modeSelect, contextSelect, curiosityPicker, cardReveal, groundRules) per OB phase.

nodes.name: OnboardingLayout.swift
nodes.summary: Shared proportional spacing constants for onboarding, expressed as fractions of live screen height/width (with a stepped nav-top) so the layout scales across the full iPhone device matrix.
nodes.languageNotes: Caseless enum aliased as OL; every metric is a fraction-of-geometry function rather than a UIScreen.main read, per the iOS 26 layout contract.

nodes.name: OnboardingLayout
nodes.summary: Static layout namespace: nav padding, vertical rhythm (compact/standard/loose), progress-bar clearance, spacer bounds, atmosphere ellipse sizing, and scroll min-height, all device-proportional.

nodes.name: CardLandingSlot.swift
nodes.summary: Pure model for one predefined card-deal landing zone: fractional center + angle with jitter fields, resolving to a concrete position/angle for any screen size.

nodes.name: CardLandingSlot
nodes.summary: Struct describing a fractional landing slot with jitter and a nested Resolved type; resolve(in:) adds randomness within the zone for an organic deal.

nodes.name: ContextOption.swift
nodes.summary: Pure data + selection model for ContextPhase cards (solo/couple × curious/in-it): first-person 'I' option sets, plus a derivedRegister that is the sole downstream behavioural driver.
nodes.languageNotes: context is the persisted routing tag while derivedRegister (anxious/excited/flexible) is what downstream branches on; accent is decorative only — never branched on.

nodes.name: ContextOption
nodes.summary: Identifiable context card model with title/subtitle/detail, a derivedRegister mapping context→SituationalRegister, and an options(appMode:stage:) resolver returning the four curated card sets.

nodes.name: CuriosityDeck.swift
nodes.summary: Pure deterministic selection model for the CuriosityPhase sort deck: round-1 feeling hands keyed by mode × register and a fixed escalating round-2 milestone set, backed by a stable id→text catalog.
nodes.languageNotes: Selection is deterministic per (mode, register) so the ConfirmationPhase editor can rebuild the exact 10 cards; catalog ids persist into onboardingData and are never reused for different text.

nodes.name: CuriosityDeck
nodes.summary: Static deck namespace: cards(round:mode:register:) maps deterministic id hands through the catalog into CuriositySortCard arrays for both sort rounds.

nodes.name: CuriositySortCard.swift
nodes.summary: Minimal Identifiable/Equatable model for a single CuriosityPhase sort card: stable id (the key written to OnboardingData), display text, and round number.

nodes.name: DemoDictionary.swift
nodes.summary: Pure mapping from the DemoPhase 'I [verb] [noun]' snapshot to an EmotionalRegister via noun keyword-stem categories modulated by the verb, defaulting to flexible — reviving the cut Compass Q3 signal.
nodes.languageNotes: Stems are matched as lowercased substrings; verb×noun triangulation flips categories (e.g. need + excited-noun → anxious) with no user-visible evaluation.

nodes.name: DemoDictionary
nodes.summary: Static register-inference namespace: category(for:) classifies the noun by stem lists, register(verb:noun:) modulates it by verb into the final EmotionalRegister.

nodes.name: FoilTear.swift
nodes.summary: Identifiable model for a single crack in the sealed BuildDeck case: face-local UV tap point, authored crack orientation, a stable procedural seed, and the strike timestamp.
nodes.languageNotes: Stored in face-local UV (not screen space) so cracks stick to the case as it floats and tilts; seed makes the branch geometry procedural yet frame-stable.

nodes.name: OnboardingData.swift
nodes.summary: Transient struct accumulating every onboarding answer (demo/name/gender/mode/experience/context/curiosity + derived opener deck) and the isReadyToComplete commit gate; written to UserProfile on completion.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents the cut CompassPhase fields (retained but never written in OB) and is the source of truth for content routing across the app lifecycle.

nodes.name: OnboardingData
nodes.summary: The onboarding aggregate struct: raw + derived fields per phase plus an isReadyToComplete computed gate requiring name, situational register, and at least one curiosity selection.

nodes.name: VaylCardModel.swift
nodes.summary: @Observable reference model for one OB canvas card: content (question/credential/face-up) plus full physics + layout state (position, rotation, scale, flip, elevation, opacity, zIndex, slot) written exclusively by VaylDirector.
nodes.languageNotes: An @Observable final class (not a struct) so the renderer reads live physics; the contract is strict write-only-by-director — no View may mutate it.

nodes.name: VaylCardModel
nodes.summary: Identifiable @Observable card model holding identity, content, and director-owned physics/layout fields that VaylCardRenderer reads to render face/back and animate flight.

nodes.name: WelcomeDeck.swift
nodes.summary: Equatable model for the forged starter deck revealed at the end of BuildDeck: real name/purpose/colorway derived from OpenerDeckType, plus a shared set of placeholder prompt cards pending the content pass.

nodes.name: WelcomeDeck
nodes.summary: Struct mapping OpenerDeckType → a named WelcomeDeck (STEADY/OPENING/RETURN/WIDER) with placeholder context cards for the reveal carousel.

nodes.name: BuildDeckPhase.swift
nodes.summary: OB BuildDeck phase View driving the multi-beat forge ceremony: the confirmed deck melts through the felt, the table works, a metallic case rises/floats, a three-strike crack ceremony shatters it into the forged WelcomeDeck reveal and founder-letter exit.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy @State async beat-sequencing with cancellable Task handles; forwards crack strikes to director.ceremony.addFoilTear and drives the persistent table bindings (rimBurst/forgeEnergy) during the forge.

nodes.name: BuildDeckPhase
nodes.summary: The forge-ceremony phase View: melt-through, case rise/float/zoom, knock anticipation, crack→shatter burst, reveal carousel, and the founder-letter peek/expand handoff to FounderLetterPhase.

nodes.name: runSequence
nodes.summary: Async Beats 1–4 driver: dealer lines, deck melt + rim oscillation, the cased deck mount/rise/float/zoom with table recede, then arming the case and starting the knock loop.

nodes.name: beginShatter
nodes.summary: Third-crack climax: runs the case overload + flood (screen flash, stage punch, celebration sparks), then resolves into the reveal carousel and unmounts the shattered case.

nodes.name: MeltThroughFelt
nodes.summary: ViewModifier that sinks the deck straight down through a fixed clip window with a soft absorption-band mask so the edge melts under the felt rather than slicing.

nodes.name: ConfirmationPhase.swift
nodes.summary: OB Confirmation phase View: deals the six credential cards out of the corner deck onto the felt along a bézier corner-sweep, opens per-card edit sheets on tap, and confirms via a fan-wide swipe-right that collapses into a deck and advances to BuildDeck.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a CornerSweep GeometryEffect for the bézier deal and a simultaneousGesture so card taps still edit while fan-level swipes commit; renders its own VaylCardFace symbol cards and never writes VaylCardModel physics.

nodes.name: ConfirmationPhase
nodes.summary: Credential-review phase View: bézier corner-sweep deal, per-credential symbol content + edit-sheet tap, damped fan drag with idle nudge, and a swipe-right commit that flips the fan face-down into a deck.

nodes.name: runEntry
nodes.summary: Async entry: projects the dealer prompt, deals the fan with a per-landing haptic tick, arms the swipe after the settle, then voices the keep-direction cue and starts the rightward nudge.

nodes.name: content
nodes.summary: Maps each OBCredential to its VaylCardContent symbol from onboardingData (snapshot shows the user's own sealed sentence; others render the phase's signature card face).

nodes.name: CornerSweep
nodes.summary: Animatable GeometryEffect translating a card along a quadratic bézier from the corner deck through a control point to its fan target as progress goes 0→1.

nodes.name: ContextPhase.swift

nodes.languageNotes: Drives an injected CarouselPhysics value type and choreographs entrance/exit timelines with cancellable Tasks; all phase advancement routed through director.concludeContext.

nodes.name: ContextPhase

nodes.name: CredentialEditorSheet.swift

nodes.name: CredentialEditorSheet

nodes.name: CuriosityPhase.swift

nodes.name: DemoPhase.swift

nodes.name: DemoPhase

nodes.name: ExperienceLevelPhase.swift

nodes.name: ExperienceLevelPhase

tour.title: Entry & Composition Root
tour.description: Start where the app starts. VaylApp.swift is the @main entry point and composition root: it constructs the shared AppState, injects it into the Store graph, wires environment objects, and kicks off session and entitlement bootstrap on launch. Reading this first gives you the runtime skeleton, every major subsystem hangs off the dependencies created here.
tour.languageLesson: A SwiftUI App conforms to the App protocol and is tagged @main. Its `body` returns a Scene (a WindowGroup), not a View. This struct is constructed once and is the single place to build app-wide singletons and inject them with .environment(...) so every child view can read them.

tour.title: Boot & Onboarding-First Routing
tour.description: Two files decide how the app comes alive. ModelContainer.swift declares SchemaV1, the single registry of every @Model type, and builds the disk-backed and in-memory SwiftData containers. AppRootView.swift is the top-level gate: it shows the splash once per cold launch, then reactively routes to onboarding, sign-in, or the main AppShell based on AppState and AuthService. This is the 'onboarding-first vs main' fork the rest of the tour branches from.
tour.languageLesson: SwiftData uses a versioned Schema and a ModelContainer as the persistence backbone (the modern replacement for Core Data's NSPersistentContainer). Registering every @Model in one SchemaV1 keeps migrations coherent; an in-memory container variant is invaluable for previews and tests.

tour.title: The Design-Token System
tour.description: Before any feature view makes sense, learn the rule everything obeys: zero raw values in Views. The App/Theme layer defines the entire visual vocabulary, AppColors, AppFonts, AppSpacing, and AppLayout, while ThemeModifiers exposes the view modifiers (cards, hairlines, glows) that views compose instead of calling primitives like .red or .shadow directly. Internalize these tokens now and every screen you read later becomes legible.
tour.languageLesson: AppLayout resolves geometry from a GeometryProxy rather than UIScreen.main.bounds, this is mandatory under the iOS 26 SDK, where global window/screen singletons are hard compiler errors. The pattern: pass `geo` down, call AppLayout.from(geo), and read screen/card dimensions from the result.

tour.title: The 4-Layer Store Architecture
tour.description: This is the architectural heart of the app, taught through the Home tab. HomeRouterView (the View) renders pixels and forwards taps, reading all of its state from HomeStore, an @Observable @MainActor class that owns state and makes decisions by calling Services. HomeDashboardView shows how a rich screen composes purely from Store state. The contract is strict: Views never touch a Service, network, or database directly, they only read Stores and call Store methods.
tour.languageLesson: @Observable (the Swift macro) replaces the older ObservableObject/@Published pattern: any stored property a View reads becomes an automatic dependency, so the View re-renders only when the exact value it used changes. Marking the Store @MainActor guarantees all state mutation happens on the main thread.

tour.title: Core Models & SwiftData Persistence
tour.description: Models are pure data shapes with no logic and no dependencies, the vocabulary every layer shares. UserProfile, Couple, and CardSession are the domain nouns the whole app revolves around (a user, the pair they form, and a completed card session). The Persistence layer, DataStore and the ModelContext extensions, wraps SwiftData reads and writes so Stores can save and fetch these @Model types without scattering raw context calls.
tour.languageLesson: A SwiftData @Model class is a reference type whose properties are transparently persisted and observed. Relationships between models (a Couple referencing two UserProfiles) are declared as plain stored properties; SwiftData infers the inverse and manages the backing store.

tour.title: Core Services & the Supabase Bridge
tour.description: Services handle network and I/O and are injected into Stores, never the reverse. This cluster is the app's connection to its backend: SupabaseManager constructs the shared SupabaseClient, SyncManager pushes local profile, identity, desire ratings, and onboarding state up to Supabase (retrying queued tasks), AuthService manages sessions, and ContentLoader loads bundled card content. AppState ties them together as the shared environment object you saw created in Step 1.
tour.languageLesson: These services lean on Swift's async/await for network calls and isolate side effects behind a clean API so Stores stay testable. SyncManager's retry-queue pattern, persist locally first, then enqueue a remote push that survives transient failures, is the app's offline-tolerant sync strategy.

tour.title: The Onboarding Director
tour.description: Now the most distinctive subsystem: the animated onboarding canvas. VaylDirector is its brain, an @Observable @MainActor coordinator (the single highest fan-in file in the codebase) that owns the current phase, every card collection and its physics state, the table fade, and crucially the only advance() path. OnboardingCanvasView renders the stage it describes, and OnboardingStage is the protocol contract between them. The non-negotiable rule: director.advance() is the ONLY way to change phase.
tour.languageLesson: VaylDirector is a state machine: phase is the single source of truth and advance() is the lone gated transition. Funneling every transition through one method (rather than letting views flip state) keeps a complex, choreographed animation sequence deterministic and replayable, the same discipline as a reducer in unidirectional-data-flow architectures.

tour.title: Onboarding Phases & Sequencers
tour.description: Each onboarding beat is its own phase view, driven by the director from Step 7. NamePhase is the dealer's explicit guided lesson that first teaches the tap-lift then swipe-up gestures; ContextPhase and BuildDeckPhase gather the user's situation and assemble their deck. GenderSequencer shows the Sequencer pattern, a per-phase choreography helper the director forwards to for timed, multi-step card motion. Together these turn the director's phase enum into the felt, on-screen experience.
tour.languageLesson: Onboarding copy always addresses one person ('you'/'I'), never 'we', because onboarding is two-device and each partner completes it alone. The Sequencer pattern decomposes a long animation into discrete, individually-tunable steps so timing can be felt and adjusted without rewriting the phase view.

tour.title: The Two-Device Card Session
tour.description: The card session is the app's most protected experience, a safe-worded, two-device flow. SessionStore is its brain: it owns card navigation, records per-card results, and persists the completed CardSession, results, and DeckProgress to SwiftData before enqueuing a Supabase sync. SessionView renders the session, and SessionPlan defines the ordered run of cards. Because it is safe-worded and stateful mid-flow, this screen is always presented as a protected cover with confirm-on-exit, never a swipe-away sheet.
tour.languageLesson: Notice the persistence ordering, save locally to SwiftData first, then enqueue the remote sync. The session survives even if the network is down, and the queued push (handled by SyncManager from Step 6) reconciles later. This 'local-first, sync-eventually' pattern recurs across every write path in the app.

tour.title: Pairing: Linking Two Devices
tour.description: Before two people can share a session they must pair. PairingStore owns the pairing flow on the client; PairingInviteView generates and shows a code for one partner, while PairingJoinView consumes a code on the other. This is the client half of the handshake, the server half (validating and consuming codes, creating the couple) lives in the edge functions you will read in Step 12. Keep that split in mind: the Store orchestrates, the backend is authoritative.

tour.title: Desire Map & Monetization
tour.description: The Desire Map is the primary conversion moment. DesireMapStore drives the rating flow where each partner privately rates desires; DesireRevealStore powers the D4 reveal, fetching the couple's alignment-only matches and resolving the free-versus-locked split against the EntitlementStore, the paywall gate. DesireRevealView stages that reveal, and PaywallSheet presents the single lifetime upgrade. The architecture deliberately ties the emotional payoff (seeing your matches) to the purchase decision.
tour.languageLesson: EntitlementStore is the client mirror of a server-authoritative truth: entitlements are granted only by a service-role edge function and never written from the client, so a jailbroken device cannot fake access. The Store reads entitlement state to gate UI, but the source of truth is the backend in Step 12.

tour.title: The Supabase Edge Functions
tour.description: The server-side brain. These Deno/TypeScript edge functions run with the service role and enforce every rule the client cannot be trusted with. create-pair and lookup-code complete the pairing handshake from Step 10 (validating, guarding against self-pair and prior couples, consuming the code). compute-desire-matches computes the server-authoritative matches behind the reveal in Step 11, grant-entitlement is the only path that grants a purchase, and get-partner resolves partner identity for the paired experience.
tour.languageLesson: Supabase edge functions are Deno runtimes: each function is an index.ts serving an HTTP handler, with imports pinned via deno.json. Running with the service-role key bypasses row-level security, so these functions are exactly where authoritative, security-sensitive logic (matching, entitlement grants, pairing consumption) must live, never in the client.

tour.title: The Database Schema
tour.description: Finish at the foundation everything rests on. The baseline migration provisions the entire backend, Postgres extensions, helper functions, and the core tables with row-level security scoped to couple membership. user_profiles is the person, couples is the pair (the FK relationship that drives every shared feature), and desire_matches stores the computed results the reveal reads. A later migration layers on the monetization entitlements table, the server-authoritative source behind the EntitlementStore from Step 11.
tour.languageLesson: Row-Level Security (RLS) policies scoped to couple membership are the backbone of privacy here: a Postgres policy ensures a user can only read rows belonging to their own couple, enforced by the database itself rather than application code. Migrations are ordered and incremental, each timestamped file evolves the schema forward in lockstep with app releases.
